title: erythrophleum laboucheri
intro: Erythrophleum laboucheri, also known as the "Naked Ebony" or "Rosewood," is a tree species native to tropical Africa. The bark of this tree is a rich source of the toxic compound erythrophleine, which has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties. Erythrophleine has been shown to have antimicrobial, antiseptic, and anti-inflammatory properties, making it useful in treating a range of health issues, including fever, diarrhea, and skin infections. The bark is typically harvested, dried, and prepared into herbal teas or infusions, as well as being used in traditional remedies for its health benefits, which include reducing fever and alleviating pain and inflammation.
